metrics.narrative: Pain is qualitatively tied to value: faster onboarding, improved compliance, reduced manual processing, and automated 3-way invoice matching are named in the CRM MEDDPICC record. Calls corroborate concrete pain: the 30 Oct 2025 commercial call surfaced that Khansaheb's property/development team suffers frequent interruptions integrating directly with the government portal, and the 30 Sep 2025 session flagged a need to assess contractor financial strength from uploaded balance sheets. None of this has been converted into a quantified dollar or hours-saved business case, and no ROI figure has been discussed with the economic buyer.

champion.narrative: Abraham Mathew, Head of Procurement, has been the constant, responsive engine of this deal since the 29 Jul 2025 first presentation: leading the POC review call on 30 Sep 2025, present at the onsite POC on 7 Oct 2025, and still the sole contact reporting back to Zycus as of the 18 May 2026 meeting. He has offered concrete help throughout (sharing sample documents, coordinating scheduling) and is the only channel currently relaying information to Bilal Hamadeh. The risk is not his advocacy but his singularity - there is no evidence of an independent Zycus line to Bilal or to Khansaheb management.

competition.narrative: Khansaheb confirmed on 16 Apr 2026 that it has shortlisted two vendors with Zycus among them, declining to name the other, and re-confirmed the two-vendor shortlist again on 18 May 2026. No evidence exists of the rival's positioning, pricing, or relative standing - only that it is still in the field. SAP S/4HANA is referenced only as Khansaheb's existing ERP and integration point, not as a competing bid.

identify_pain.narrative: Pain is well established and multi-sourced: manual, document-heavy vendor onboarding (KYC/AML) across multiple entities; manual invoice processing; lack of real-time supplier visibility (CRM MEDDPICC); a stated need to validate expiring trade licenses and VAT certificates automatically (call 2025-09-30); and direct government/land-department portal integration failures causing operational interruption (call 2025-10-30, described by a Khansaheb attendee as needing 'minimal interruption and failure points').

paper_process.narrative: An NDA was initiated on 29 Jul 2025 following the first head-of-procurement presentation, and a minimum 3-year contract term was discussed and accepted in principle on 30 Oct 2025 ('the business won't do anything less than 3 years'). No MSA, SOW, or formal order form work has begun - the deal never progressed past commercial estimation before the scope narrowed and the regional freeze intervened, so there is no active paper trail today.

economic_buyer.narrative: Bilal Hamadeh is consistently named as the GM across the CRM MEDDPICC record and the deal narrative, and he had direct engagement in 2025 - demoed to on 7 Aug 2025 ('very engaged') and present at the 14 Oct 2025 onsite as Exec Sponsor. However, he has had no confirmed direct Zycus conversation since that onsite: the rep escalated to him in Feb 2026 without success, and the 18 May 2026 meeting note explicitly states he was unavailable again, with Abraham Mathew relaying that he 'will talk to the relevant stakeholder to power is identified but currently indirect only.

decision_process.narrative: The process is understood in shape: a phased delivery was agreed on 30 Oct 2025 - Phase 1 covering supplier management (iSupplier) and eInvoicing as a pilot, with sourcing and contract management scoped as a separate later project. Per that same call, the pilot 'will go to management (via Bilal) for review and approval,' with the rep on the call stating explicitly there would be 'no false commitments at this stage.' The outcome of that management review has never been confirmed back to Zycus, and the process has since been overtaken by the regional spend freeze reported from 25 Mar 2026 onward.

decision_criteria.narrative: Criteria are well documented and corroborated by calls: vendor self-registration with KYC/AML validation, 3-way invoice matching, ERP integration, automation of manual approvals, document/compliance validation (trade license, VAT, Emirates ID expiry checks), and cost efficiency. The 14 Oct 2025 full-platform demo walked through each of these against Khansaheb's own documents (registration form, confidentiality undertaking, category/value matrix), confirming the criteria are grounded in the buyer's actual requirements, not generic.

items.name: New Zycus RVP introduces himself to Abraham
items.summary: Mohamad Alhakim (Zycus RVP Middle East) emailed Abraham to introduce himself as the new primary contact after Dan's departure, signaling he's reviewed the deal history and is ready to pick up discussions; he's offering to meet in person or virtually but the next move is on Abraham to signal timing.

day_summary.overall: After a period of stalled contact following the previous rep Dan's departure, Zycus re-engaged Khansaheb with a new point of contact taking over the account. The new RVP introduced himself directly to Abraham, acknowledged the external challenges that had paused the deal, and put the ball in Khansaheb's court to resume talks when ready — so the deal now sits waiting on Abraham to respond.

cro_panel.intro: A genuinely qualified, deeply-demoed deal (custom onsite POC, full-platform walkthrough, phased commercial plan) that has been knocked off its pace twice; once by a scope cut to near-sub-scale size in Oct 2025, and again by a regional conflict/blockade freezing all non-essential spend since roughly March 2026.; The only live thread is a single, responsive champion with no confirmed access to the economic buyer since the same freeze began

deal.next_step: Confidence 50% 18th May - Met the HOP/Champion (Abraham, - Bilal the EB was unavailable) - he confirmed we are shortlisted against one other provider, their current thinking is that all non-essential spend is paused until later in the year whilst the conflict/blockade continues (new construction projects are paused, though they have several years of projects underway) - their current concern is the materials prices have sky-rocketed due to the blockade & fuel prices - I have planted some seeds around my blog post on investing in procuretech to protect their margins & unlock better materials pricing), offered to present to upper mgt and/or bring Carl in in helpful to show how we can help strengthen their economics, he will talk to Bilal. 16th April - Project continues to be on pause due to the war, but expecting to resume, confirmed they had shortlisted two vendors and Zycus is one, (wouldn't say who else) - current expectation is that they will resume in Q2 - moving back to end Q2. 25th Mar - Response that the project is paused due to the war priorities: "Given the present conditions, we have a delay in the closure of this project. We will provide further updates should there be any changes." appears we are still in the mix as one of their options. 13th Mar - Reached out again for an update (several out of office responses) and requested a meeting after the Eid break. 17th Feb - Same response, not interested in meeting up... "Thank you for your patience, the project is still on and under review with our consultant. The team will reach out to you as & when there are new developments". 16th Feb - Gone quiet since they refocussed on a reduced scope, escalated to senior EB requesting a f2f this week, reducing confidence to 5%. 16th Jan - Pushing to get a meeting while Tanya is in town end Jan/early Feb, Abraham will get back to us. 20th Dec - Pushed a couple of times for updates - response 'management are evaluating, will update in the new year' 19th Nov - As expected, when they provided the usage for the pilot it's too low, rather than walk away I have added some modules they were interested in and pitched 2 scenarios as our 'min prepared to support', still a small deal but could be acceptable, let's see their response, if negative its dead. Dropped confidence to 5%. 30th Oct - Commercial discussion held, changes

confidence_signals.summary: Buyer engagement remains live per Salesforce (activity logged 3 Jul 2026, 6 days ago; a genuine two-way buyer email arrived 19 May 2026), but the rep's own next-step confidence read has been volatile - dropping to 5% in Nov 2025 amid a scope-driven near-disqualification and only partially recovering to 50% by 18 May 2026 - while the regional blockade continues to suppress the buyer's ability to commit.

north_star_verdict.math: Close date 2026-08-31, today 2026-07-08, 54 days to close. Stage Shortlisted, dwell since roughly late 2025 through repeated re-scoping (Q4 2025 -> Q1 2026 -> end-Q2 2026 -> 31 Aug 2026 close). Rep confidence has swung 10% (Oct 2025) -> 5% (Nov 2025/Feb 2026) -> 50% (18 May 2026) on verbal reassurance only, with no dated milestone locked and no EB contact in over 7 months.
evidence: Next_Step_History__c, 18 May 2026: 'confirmed we are shortlisted against one other provider, their current thinking is that all non-essential spend is paused until later in the year whilst the conflict/blockade continues'
Next_Step_History__c, 16 Apr 2026: 'confirmed they had shortlisted two vendors and Zycus is one... current expectation is that they will resume in Q2'
Next_Step_History__c, 16 Feb 2026: confidence cut to 5% after buyer went quiet
north_star_verdict.headline: Zycus is still one of two shortlisted providers, but the deal is stalled: economic buyer Bilal Hamadeh has been unreachable since the Oct 2025 onsite and a regional conflict/blockade has frozen Khansaheb's non-essential spend with no lift date, against a close date only 54 days away.

flags: Single-threaded through Abraham Mathew with no independent line to economic buyer Bilal Hamadeh since the 14 Oct 2025 onsite - open a second channel (via Ravi Shankar/Crowe or a direct exec-to-exec outreach) before the deal becomes fully dependent on one contact.

Relationship-continuity risk: Dan Quinn (11 months of built history with Abraham) departed and Mohamad Alhakim took over 29 Jun 2026 with no reply yet to his 3 Jul introduction - a warm, context-rich handoff call is needed to preserve the relationship equity built over the past year.

The rival vendor named in the 2-vendor shortlist has never been identified and no competitive refresh has run since 18 May 2026 (>30 days) - ask Abraham directly who it is and reinforce the Merlin/single-platform wedge against Khansaheb's fragmented 4-ERP environment.
Reconfirm the compelling event: get a written estimate of when the spend freeze lifts so the 31 Aug 2026 close date and Best Case forecast can be recalibrated honestly.
The competitive shortlist has only ever named 'one other provider' without identifying it; use Abraham to learn who it is and where they are weaker before pricing conversations resume.

The economic buyer (Bilal Hamadeh) has not engaged Zycus directly since the Oct 2025 POC; converting Abraham's 18 May 2026 offer to broker a Bilal conversation into an actual meeting is the highest-leverage move to unstick the deal.

The pilot has been scoped down to a single business unit that Khansaheb itself called 'on the cusp of being too small'; reinforcing the group-wide phase-two story discussed on the 30 Oct 2025 commercial call would help justify the deal's size to the review committee.
Convert Abraham's own offer to present to upper management into a scheduled executive session with Carl Kimball before the blockade-driven freeze causes the pilot to be shelved indefinitely.
Reconfirm competitive standing against the unnamed second shortlisted vendor once evaluation resumes, since Khansaheb has given no read on the two finalists since the process paused in late 2025.

Champion Abraham Mathew has never delivered direct EB access to Bilal Hamadeh in nearly a year; an executive-to-executive connect via the deal owner's manager is now the single highest-leverage lever to unstick this deal.

The pilot scope has shrunk to iSupplier plus eInvoicing only, which both sides flagged as sub-scale (30 Oct 2025); anchoring the business case to the blockade-driven materials-cost pain Abraham raised on 18 May 2026 is the strongest current path to re-justify the investment.

With only one named rival ('Others') and no visibility into their standing, using the next Abraham contact to press for even directional competitive detail would sharpen the win read before spend unfreezes.
Re-confirm the regional spend-freeze status directly rather than waiting on the next incidental update - the close date depends entirely on a resumption date that has never been given.
best_practice_check.summary: The core lever is access: a genuinely qualified, well-demoed deal is running through a single contact with no confirmed line to the person who actually approves it.

customer_preference.evidence: Abraham Mathew told the rep on 18 May 2026 that Khansaheb has shortlisted two vendors with Zycus among them; no stated preference for Zycus over the rival has been voiced by the buyer.

competitive_position.summary: Khansaheb has held Zycus and one unnamed provider ('Others' in Salesforce) as the two finalists since at least Apr 2026, reconfirmed again on 18 May 2026 - the buyer will not disclose the rival's identity. This is the single live competitive thread; SAP S/4HANA is Khansaheb's existing ERP and only an integration point, not a competing bid, and has faded from relevance as a threat entirely.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: A genuinely qualified, deeply-demoed deal (custom onsite POC, full-platform walkthrough, phased commercial plan) that has been knocked off its pace twice: once by a scope cut to near-sub-scale size in Oct 2025, and again by a regional conflict/blockade freezing all non-essential spend since roughly March 2026. The only live thread is a single, responsive champion with no confirmed access to the economic buyer since the same freeze began.

deal_risk.text: Single-threaded relationship - Zycus has no independent line to Bilal Hamadeh or Khansaheb management if Abraham Mathew deprioritizes the deal.

Unresolved regulatory dependency - the eInvoicing scope depends on UAE FDA-portal integration via a nominated third-party intermediary whose compliance timeline was still unresolved as of the 30 Oct 2025 commercial call.

win_position.text: Shortlisted against a single named rival - Abraham Mathew confirmed on 18 May 2026 that Khansaheb has narrowed to two vendors with Zycus in the final field, though the rival's identity was withheld.

Economic buyer disengaged from direct conversation - Bilal Hamadeh was unavailable at the 18 May 2026 meeting, continuing a pattern with no direct Zycus-Bilal contact confirmed since the 14 Oct 2025 onsite POC.

External freeze threatens the close date - a regional conflict/blockade is pausing Khansaheb's non-essential spend with no resumption date named as of 18 May 2026, directly bearing on the 31 Aug 2026 close.

deal_momentum.text: Rep-rated confidence has been volatile - it fell to 5% in Nov 2025 after a scope-driven near-disqualification and had only partially recovered to 50% by 18 May 2026.
The deal is still technically alive - Salesforce logs activity as recently as 3 Jul 2026 (6 days ago) and a genuine two-way buyer email arrived 19 May 2026.
No new forward-dated milestone since 18 May 2026 (~7.5 weeks) - the last logged commitment was Abraham relaying the conversation to Bilal, with no confirmed follow-up date.

customer_commitment.text: Scope has shrunk to near sub-scale - the 30 Oct 2025 commercial call agreed a single-business-unit pilot (iSupplier + eInvoicing only) that Khansaheb itself called 'on the cusp of being too small.'
Abraham Mathew has remained an engaged, responsive champion throughout, sharing sample documents and coordinating scheduling even through the slowdown.

hard.next_step: Confidence 50% 18th May - Met the HOP/Champion (Abraham, - Bilal the EB was unavailable) - he confirmed we are shortlisted against one other provider, their current thinking is that all non-essential spend is paused until later in the year whilst the conflict/blockade continues (new construction projects are paused, though they have several years of projects underway) - their current concern is the materials prices have sky-rocketed due to the blockade & fuel prices - I have planted some seeds around my blog post on investing in procuretech to protect their margins & unlock better materials pricing), offered to present to upper mgt and/or bring Carl in in helpful to show how we can help strengthen their economics, he will talk to Bilal. 16th April - Project continues to be on pause due to the war, but expecting to resume, confirmed they had shortlisted two vendors and Zycus is one, (wouldn't say who else) - current expectation is that they will resume in Q2 - moving back to end Q2. 25th Mar - Response that the project is paused due to the war priorities: "Given the present conditions, we have a delay in the closure of this project. We will provide further updates should there be any changes."<span style="font-family: Tahoma,sans-serif; font-size: 11pt;"> appears we are still in the mix as one of their options. 13th Mar - Reached out again for an update (several out of office responses) and requested a meeting after the Eid break. 17th Feb - Same response, not interested in meeting up... "Thank you for your patience, the project is still on and under review with our consultant. The team will reach out to you as & when there are new developments". 16th Feb - Gone quiet since they refocussed on a reduced scope, escalated to senior EB requesting a f2f this week, reducing confidence to 5%. 16th Jan - Pushing to get a meeting while Tanya is in town end Jan/early Feb, Abraham will get back to us. 20th Dec - Pushed a couple of times for updates - response 'management are evaluating, will update in the new year' 19th Nov - As expected, when they provided the usage for the pilot it's too low, rather than walk away I have added some modules they were interested in and pitched 2 scenarios as our 'min prepared to support', still a small deal but could be acceptable, let's see their response, if negative its dead. Dropped confidence to 5%. 30th Oct - Commercial discussion held, changes in approach, now looking to run a pilot approach to a business unit before deploying to wider business, reduces the scope and also changed sourcing to a later phase - now on the cusp of being too small, will get clarity once there are eInvoicing estimates of usage - moved close date back to Q4 and confidence down to 10% 28th Oct - Call scheduled Thurs 30th to discuss commercial estimation. 22nd Oct - Nudged to see if they need help with completing the commercial estimation together. 14th Oct - POC went well, they are asking for pricing, have shared the estimation template and offer to run through it together. 13th Oct - POC with Bilal (Exec Sponsor) confirmed onsite scheduled for 14th Oct (with Tanya & Dan onsite) 7th Oct - POC held onsite with Khansaheb senior manager (Abraham) and Crowe consultants, IF successful they will escalate to another session with Bilal (Exec sponsor) 23rd Sept - Pre-POC meeting set for 29th to review POC prior to live demo session (later that week TBC). 11th Sept - POC details shared to run a live demo.... pilots team tied up for next few weeks Tanya will have to build delaying the process. 25th Aug - No response from Ravi, called and postponed (also not clear Abraham is back yet) - some option offered, no response yet. 14th Aug - Ravi scheduled 26th but we have a clash, requested to reschedule. 7th Aug - Demo'ed to Bilal (GM) and exec sponsor, went well, very engaged - next step to build a POC demo based on their processes when Abrahim returns on 26th (maybe run it on 10th when Tanya/Carl are in Dubai? TBC). 31st July - Ravi called to say that the GM (Bilal) doesn't want to wait for Abraham's response - demo being arranged for next Weds-Fri. 29th July - Presented to Abraham (HOP), went well, feedback was positive, next steps are: follow up demo on invoicing & Sourcing, to sign an NDA, to get a proposal and present to management prior to a decision, shared the pricing estimate template and NDA underway. - moved to Formal Evaluation. 22nd July - Following demo request had a disco call with their consultant (Ravi - Crowe UAE) managing the project, interested in S2C but priority focus is supplier management- next step is to present to head of procurement, will be presenting iSupplier/ZSN, iRisk and iSource- scheduled for 29th Jul20
